item.title: Newcastle Jets claim maiden A-League Men's premiership after Central Coast shocks Auckland
item.description: Newcastle has claimed a maiden A-League Men's Premiers Plate after local rivals Central Coast stunned second-placed Auckland.

item.title: This week in history quiz, April 20: Can you tame our tricky questions?
item.description: Is two-up actually illegal in Australia? And what movie beat Crocodile Dundee to the top of the box office in 1986?

item.title: With Iran and the Strait of Hormuz still in flux, China's bet on renewable energy is paying off
item.description: While the rest of us count the cost in higher fuel costs, higher inflation and a weaker economy, China — the actual winner of the war — will be enjoying a surge of global interest in its solar panels, wind turbines and electric vehicles.

item.title: Australia's 'lucky country' mantle has slipped — and we're not happy about it
item.description: For the first time in this survey, more Australians are dissatisfied with the direction of the country than satisfied. The conflict between the US/Israel and Iran has delivered more than geopolitical uncertainty.

item.title: Can you ask the council to cut down a tree?
item.description: Expert advice on how to handle council trees causing problems on private property.

item.title: Peter and Rosie use power of song and an appearance on The Piano to save endangered language
item.description: Peter is the last living speaker of the First Nations language Thiinma. When he met linguist Rosie, they used music to help keep his knowledge alive, and formed a beautiful friendship along the way.

item.title: ADHD rates in adults are skyrocketing — but by how much depends where you live
item.description: ADHD diagnoses in adults have exploded across Australia since 2017. For the first time, we have the data that shows where it is — and isn't — happening.

item.title: Netball star Jo Weston calls for pay increase after new broadcast deal
item.description: Diamonds and Melbourne Vixens star Jo Weston calls for a pay increase after a new free-to-air broadcast deal.

item.title: Strait of Hormuz closure has Russia poised to capitalise on global hunger crisis
item.description: With fears hunger could hit record levels, Vladimir Putin and Russia are poised to capitalise — financially and diplomatically.

item.title: Sydney kids turn struggle with school into ambitious art show
item.description: At an art gallery in Sydney's inner west, a group of marginalised children are staging their first exhibition — and for many of them, it is the first time they have felt seen.

item.title: US delegation led by Vance to go to Pakistan for Iran peace talks, says Trump
item.description: A US delegation will head to Pakistan for potential peace talks, Donald Trump says, adding that he assumes "nobody's playing games".

item.title: How ex-NRL star Zac Lomax fared in first Super Rugby Pacific start for Western Force
item.description: Even if Zac Lomax's starting debut for the Western Force had been disastrous, it would still deserve to be considered a success.

item.title: Tasmanian Labor says it's 'back' after multiple election defeats, but there's still a long road ahead
item.description: The Labor party's first state conference in seven years reunited old friends and potentially a few "frenemies", but with issues like greyhound racing absent, drama was largely kept at bay.

item.title: Lifeblood's blood donation eligibility expands for gay and bisexual men, transgender people
item.description: From today, Australian Red Cross Lifeblood will use gender-neutral assessments for blood and platelets donations.

item.title: Araluen seeks to solve mystery of World War I digger C Gordon
item.description: The names etched into the Araluen War Memorial are read aloud every Anzac Day, but one digger from World War I remains a mystery.

item.title: Australia women lose Hong Kong rugby sevens final to New Zealand, men finish sixth
item.description: The Black Ferns Sevens beat Australia's women's rugby sevens team to win the Hong Kong title, marking the ninth time in the past 10 finals that the Kiwis have come out on top.

item.title: Illicit tobacco report ranks NT 'worst performing' jurisdiction, says 'real penalties' are needed
item.description: Smoking is one of the leading causes of death in the Northern Territory. Now, the jurisdiction has been ranked the weakest in the country for how it tackles cheap, illicit tobacco.

item.title: Public health researcher David Thomas wants the NT to introduce laws tackling illicit tobacco
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Grocery prices set to surge due to shortages caused by war in Iran
item.description: Farmers ask for a 30-cent-per-litre price rise for milk as fuel and fertiliser prices surge.

item.title: Falling consumption, rising costs and tariffs take bounce out of yellow tail winemaker
item.description: The maker of one of Australia's best-known wine brands, yellow tail, says rising costs and global factors such as tariffs are behind the sharp drop in profits.

item.title: ASX edges higher despite rising Hormuz tension — as it happened
item.description: The ASX 200 clawed back earlier, steeper losses to close marginally higher with demand for retailers and banks outweighing selling in energy stocks and miners.

item.title: Service club wants legislation changed after RSL refuses Anzac Day two-up request
item.description: An ex-services organisation wants changes to Queensland legislation that allows the local RSL branch to reject its application to play two-up on Anzac Day.

item.title: The ABC Classic 100 in Concert brings audience music picks to the concert hall and people's homes
item.description: The ABC Classic 100 in Concert brings Australia's most popular classical music, as chosen by audiences, from the radio to the stage.

item.title: Plans emerge for country's biggest AI data centre in Western Australia
item.description: A huge artificial intelligence data centre capable of training models like ChatGPT is planned for WA's remote north at a cost of billons of dollars.

item.title: Cameron Green victim of miracle stumping in IPL, Cooper Connolly powers Punjab Kings to another win
item.description: The losing run for Cameron Green's Kolkata in the IPL finally ends, but the Australian is undone by a spectacular stumping from Dhruv Jurel, while compatriot Cooper Connolly explodes for the Punjab Kings.

item.title: Touring artists wind back shows as fuel crisis bites
item.description: Adele Oliver admits being a country music artist has "never been a great career choice", but she says things are worse than ever, with her last gig costing her money due to the escalating price of fuel.

item.title: Shane Flanagan out as St George Illawarra Dragons coach after winless start to NRL season
item.description: Shane Flanagan and the St George Illawarra Dragons part ways after a winless start to 2026 extends an 11-game losing streak dating back to last August.

item.title: Melbourne council workers and Victorian teachers to strike during budget week
item.description: More than 1,000 workers across seven local councils in Melbourne are set to walk off the job next month on the same day the Victorian government releases its budget, with garbage collection to be impacted by the strike.

item.title: Grocery prices set to surge due to war in Iran
item.description: Listen to ABC News interviews and commentary and analysis from radio programs like AM, PM and The World Today.

item.title: Kakadu Aboriginal corporation to take over as operator of Jabiru Airport
item.description: Jabiru Airport had been slated for demolition in October this year, but after months of uncertainty, a new operator has emerged to take over.

item.title: US capture of Iranian cargo ship puts ceasefire at risk ahead of peace talks
item.description: Donald Trump says the Iranian-flagged ship ignored warnings from the US Navy, but Iran says the action is piracy and breaches the ceasefire terms.

item.title: Super Netball Round-Up: How many games will be shown on free-to-air TV in the Nine broadcast deal?
item.description: Uncertainties over the details of Netball Australia's broadcast agreement with Nine left fans hesitant to celebrate. Now we reveal how many games will actually be seen on free-to-air TV.

item.title: Inquest into shooting death of Sydney police accountant Curtis Cheng begins
item.description: A coroner will examine what authorities knew about 15-year-old gunman Farhad Jabar in the lead-up to the attack.

item.title: Removal of 88-year-old giant sequoias begins in Hobart's St David's Park
item.description: Two iconic giant sequoias, planted to mark the coronation of King George VI and Queen Elizabeth, are being removed from a Hobart park from Monday after their health rapidly declined.

item.title: Potentially 800,000 year old Australite found in WA Goldfields
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: WA prospector discovers rare cosmic debris in 'the middle of nowhere'
item.description: The object formed when a meteorite slammed into Earth more than 800,000 years ago.

item.title: Soaring airfares and reduced flights force some Australians to reconsider holiday and work plans
item.description: The war in the Middle East has caused chaos across some of the world's busiest aviation regions, with many Australians now reconsidering their travel plans — or abandoning them altogether.

item.title: Norman Griffiths Oval project blows out to $20 million five years after commencing
item.description: Five years ago the conversion of a washout-prone natural field to synthetic should've cost $3.3 million. Project changes and a legal dispute with the old contractor has blown out the price.

item.title: Fast and satisfying dinners for your busiest evenings
item.description: From frittata to one-pot pasta, these speedy dinners are perfect for busy evenings and worth adding to your regular rotation.

item.title: Drivers are choosing EVs amid the fuel crisis but is the infrastructure ready?
item.description: With no end in sight to soaring fuel prices, 7.30 decided to take an EV for a test drive to see how far it could take our reporter, and if Australia's EV charging network stacks up.

item.title: Parilla Premium Potatoes convicted, fined $360k after plane propeller 'severely damaged' employee's arm
item.description: A Southern Mallee Potato company has been convicted and fined $360,000 after a plane propeller struck an employee's arm, leaving him with multiple injuries, including partial finger amputations.

item.title: For St George Illawarra, Shane Flanagan's dismissal is a new version of the same old story
item.description: St George Illawarra might have sacked coach Shane Flanagan amid a record losing streak, but such struggles have become sadly familiar to the joint venture.

item.title: Brisbane Broncos injury list grows, with Payne Haas to miss up to eight weeks, and Corey Jensen in hospital
item.description: Brisbane's injury crisis escalates, with Payne Haas to miss six to eight weeks and fellow prop Corey Jensen in hospital with a blood clot.

item.title: Woman lied to police about brother’s alleged fatal poisoning by mother Maree Crabtree for fear of jail, jury hears
item.description: Maree Mavis Crabtree is fighting three charges at a trial in the Supreme Court in Brisbane, including the murder and attempted murder of her son Jonathan Crabtree.

item.title: Tow truck driver fined $5,000 over 2023 crash that killed man on Forrest Highway
item.description: Ross David Gamage has been fined $5,000 and had his licence suspended for six months over a 2023 crash that killed a 45-year-old man near Waroona.

item.title: Former footballer Barry Cable found not guilty of sexually abusing child in 1960s
item.description: Former Australian Rules footballer Barry Cable has been found not guilty of sexually abusing a child in Perth in the 1960s.

item.title: Rebel Wilson accused of defaming actor with sexual harassment claim
item.description: Hollywood star Rebel Wilson is being sued in the Federal Court for defamation by one of the lead actor's in her directorial debut film, The Deb.

item.title: AFL apologises after Butters appeal upheld, umpire abuse conviction quashed
item.description: The AFL appeals board has quashed Zak Butters's conviction for abusive language against umpire Nick Foot, saying a Tribunal member listening in while driving his car for part of the original hearing constitutes a miscarriage of justice.

item.title: Cage fighter threatened pregnant girlfriend after killing best friend, murder trial told
item.description: A professional cage fighter accused of murdering his best friend threatened to kill his then-pregnant girlfriend, a Sydney jury has heard.

item.title: Seeking an ADHD diagnosis as an adult? This is how assessments are meant to work
item.description: Who can diagnose ADHD, what a proper assessment should include, and the red flags to look out for.

item.title: Gavin Preston's accused killer searched for 'no extradition countries' after shooting, prosecutors say
item.description: The Supreme Court has been told of the alleged internet searches made by a man accused of a brazen daylight cafe shooting in Melbourne's north-west.

item.title: Sandipan Dhar's father says someone should be held accountable for son's death
item.description: The father of Sandipan Dhar, who died after not receiving a blood test at Joondalup Health Campus, says there is still a lack of accountability over his son's death.

item.title: Mystery surrounds prosthetic limb found on NSW Mid North Coast beach
item.description: The prosthetic leg has been handed to police after being discovered by a couple while they were fishing on a NSW Mid North Coast beach.

item.title: Monday finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Humanoid robots race in Beijing's robot half-marathon
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Terminally ill woman awarded $1.6 million in case against former teacher Stephen Fane Noga
item.description: A Tasmanian judge has awarded a woman who is now terminally ill $1.6 million in damages after she sued her former school teacher over allegations of repeated sexual abuse in the 1980s.

item.title: Teenager charged with toilet block assault took 'calculated steps' to lure child inside, court hears
item.description: Prosecutors say the allegations against a teenage boy accused of sexually assaulting a child in a toilet block are so serious he should face trial in an adult court.

item.title: Inquest hears warnings missed before mother's death in Darwin domestic violence shelter
item.description: An inquest has heard harrowing details of the final moments of a 33-year-old mother who bled to death after harming herself inside a Darwin domestic violence shelter.

item.title: Carlton confirms Elijah Hollands is in hospital after concerning scenes on Thursday night
item.description: Carlton says young star Elijah Hollands has been admitted to hospital after suffering a mental health episode in Thursday night's match.

item.title: Waves swell in Hokkaido, Japan after magnitude 7.5 earthquake
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Keir Starmer says he was 'wrong' to appoint Mandelson to DC ambassador role
item.description: The crisis around the embattled British prime minister continues to grow, with Sir Keir Starmer facing questions from MPs about what he knew of the vetting process of Peter Mandelson.

item.title: US singer D4vd pleads not guilty to murdering 14yo girl found in his Tesla
item.description: The 21-year-old, who rose to fame after going viral on TikTok, has pleaded not guilty to a murder charge of a 14-year-old whose dismembered body was found in his seemingly abandoned Tesla.

item.title: Trump warns US military 'ready to go' as US-Iran peace talks loom — as it happened
item.description: US President Donald Trump has warned if a deal with Iran is not reached soon, US military action could resume.

item.title: Disabled NZ parrot uses what's left of his beak to dominate rivals
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: ABC Places quiz: Where in the world are we?
item.description: Today's ABC Places quiz features photos from 10 locations around the world. Your challenge is to guess where you are.

item.title: Six ways your smartwatch is lying to you, according to science
item.description: Wearable fitness technologies like smartwatches are worn by millions of people daily. But they're not as accurate as you might think.

item.title: How to keep your dog happy living in an apartment
item.description: Providing opportunities for your dog to explore, giving them a safe and comfortable space to retreat to — as well as a good routine — are key to keeping your dog happy.

item.title: ACCC accuses Woolworths of misleading pricing practices as next Federal Court case kicks off
item.description: The sequel to Australia's supermarket pricing wars is about to kick off in the Federal Court as the ACCC takes on Woolworths over what it argues are misleading pricing practices.

item.title: Labor faces gas tax pressure as inquiry heats up before budget
item.description: Calls are growing for a new 25 per cent tax on gas exports to increase the petroleum resource rent tax on windfall profits, but companies have warned any changes would jeopardise Australia's energy supply.

item.title: Australian women have overtaken men in prescribing rates for ADHD medication
item.description: Within the sharp increase of ADHD rates in Australia lies a hidden story — more women are being prescribed medication than men. Understanding why is more complicated.

item.title: Why Australia's big gas producers haven't jacked up domestic prices unlike during Ukraine war
item.description: After a decade and a half of mostly failed threats from three prime ministers and constant surveillance by the competition regulator, the east coast gas export giants are suddenly putting Australians first.

item.title: From cost blowouts to fraud and benefits, here's everything you need to know about the NDIS
item.description: With more changes surrounding the NDIS just announced, here’s everything you need to know about the scheme.

item.title: NRL limits contact in training in bid to reduce head injuries
item.description: The move to restrict training loads is aimed at mitigating injuries, including player head injuries, which continue to be a major issue for all contact sports.

item.title: From TikTok to Tongan prison: Australian deportee who imported drugs and called himself a Comanchero boss
item.description: Eneasi Taumoefolau's 2024 arrest on drugs charges ignited fears the notoriously violent outlaw Comancheros gang had infiltrated "the Friendly Islands".

item.title: In charts: Australia's relationship with cash over the past two decades
item.description: A new report has found cash use has dropped by more than 50 per cent in almost 20 years. But there has been a slight increase in cash payments since 2022.

item.title: Victor Wembanyama's post-season entrance among leading storylines as 2026 NBA playoffs begin
item.description: The first round of the 2026 NBA Playoffs is officially underway. Here are the key things to look out for in each series.

item.title: Administrators for Liberty Bell Bay smelter optimistic buyer will be found, as workers face job decision
item.description: With Australian and international bidders apparently eyeing off the troubled Liberty Bell Bay smelter, the workers and their families just want to know they'll have a job at the end of it all.

item.title: Students thank Australian Defence Force veterans with postcards ahead of Anzac Day
item.description: Thousands of children across Queensland are sending postcards to former and serving Australian Defence Force members.

item.title: Volunteers who helped find Celine Cremer plan new missing person search in Tasmanian wilderness
item.description: After helping find missing tourist Celine Cremer, some of the search volunteers plan to formalise their group and take on other missing persons cases, including that of a man who went missing last year.

item.title: Native title holders say they have been sidelined by Rex Minerals over SA copper mine
item.description: A First Nations group on South Australia's Yorke Peninsula says a mining company has bulldozed areas of cultural significance and has started development without a formal agreement with Traditional Owners, but the company says it has "continuously engaged" with the group.

item.title: Indonesian fisher says poverty behind illegal fishing in Australian waters
item.description: Why do Indonesian crews keep fishing illegally in Australian waters when the activity is so perilous?

item.title: Community calls for water quality over quantity, in Murray-Darling Basin Plan review
item.description: The first-ever review into the Murray-Darling Basin Plan is underway, and the St George community wants a better deal.

item.title: Apple CEO Tim Cook to step down and hand reins to iPhone maker's hardware leader
item.description: Apple has announced the chief executive who took over from Steve Jobs is to step down and has named his successor.

item.title: Dean Young named St George Illawarra Dragons coach after Shane Flanagan's exit
item.description: After about 12 hours without a head coach, the Dragons appoint assistant Dean Young to lead the winless side for "at least" the rest of the 2026 NRL season.

item.title: Turtles 'awaiting their fate' buried under mud at NSW wetlands after inflows halted
item.description: Researchers in north-west NSW are wading through thick mud in an attempt to save hundreds of turtles after the NSW government decided to stop supplying water to the protected wetland.

item.title: Turtles buried in mud at Gwydir Wetlands
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Woolworths fronts court on dodgy discount allegations, ASX slips — as it happened
item.description: Supermarket giant Woolworths faced off with the consumer watchdog in court on Tuesday. The ASX 200 slipped into the red. Catch up on the day's events on the ABC News live markets blog.

item.title: Australian War Memorial curator amazed to find 4 metres of WWII 'short snorter' banknotes
item.description: It is a crime to deface banknotes, but these rare wartime notes covered in scribbles cause excitement whenever they appear.

item.title: US, Philippine and Australian troops begin largest military exercise in 'real-world conditions'
item.description: Troops from the US, Philippines, Australia and Japan unite for the largest military exercise in "real-world conditions" to ensure security in the Indo-Pacific region. But China has criticised the drill.

item.title: Mount Gambier locals hope for rail return following Qantas flight suspension
item.description: Trains stopped coming to Mount Gambier decades ago, but with fewer flights, locals want them back.

item.title: New Zealand urges Wellington residents in low-lying areas to evacuate due to floods
item.description: People living in low-lying areas of New Zealand's capital city, Wellington, are urged to evacuate as heavy rain drenches the city.

item.title: Formula 1 to make rule changes for Miami Grand Prix after concerns over new engine regulations
item.description: The next Formula 1 race in Miami will be a testing and proving ground for a number of new rules aimed at improving performance and safety.

item.title: US presses Cuba for reforms on political freedoms in meeting held in Havana
item.description: Cuba says the exchange was conducted "respectfully and professionally" and that the US delegation did not issue any threats or deadlines.

item.title: SA childcare centre forced to close for three months over seven supervision incidents
item.description: Another Adelaide childcare centre has been forced to shut its doors for three months after seven incidents of children being left unsupervised.

item.title: Homes damaged, powerlines down in Bunbury as possible tornado or microburst rips through city
item.description: Winds of more than 100 kilometres an hour swept along WA's southern coast last night, with serious damage reported in Bunbury and Esperance.

item.title: Who is John Ternus, the hardware engineer appointed as new CEO of Apple?
item.description: The new CEO of Apple will be tasked with catching up in the AI race and continuing the company's legacy as a dominating force in the technology world.

item.title: New Zealand Prime Minister Christopher Luxon survives confidence vote
item.description: New Zealand Prime Minister Christopher Luxon says he has the support of his party after calling and winning a vote of confidence in his leadership.

item.title: What I learnt about myself after quitting TV for a month
item.description: It was partly an experiment in self-control and habit-breaking, but also a response to the unhappy realisation that watching shows had become my crutch to zone out.

item.title: Dean Harriss to support Tasmania's greyhound racing ban if compensation package increased
item.description: Upper house independent Dean Harriss, who is up for re-election next month, says he will support the Tasmanian government's ban on greyhound racing, but only if the industry compensation package is increased.

item.title: Four educators plead not guilty to assaulting students at Queanbeyan school
item.description: Four educators accused of assaulting children, aged 9 and 10, at a Queanbeyan school were not present in court when their lawyers indicated they would fight the charges.

item.title: Second death of Aboriginal inmate in Bandyup Women's Prison sparks call for review
item.description: Calls are mounting for a "transparent" government review of WA's Bandyup Women's Prison after a second Aboriginal inmate dies inside the facility in less than five weeks.

item.title: About 2,000 fines withdrawn since AI road safety cameras introduced in WA
item.description: About 2,000 fines valued at $1.1 million issued as a result of AI-assisted road safety cameras in WA have been withdrawn, six months after the devices were introduced.

item.title: Elijah Hollands's dad posts message about Carlton player's mental health struggle
item.description: Elijah Hollands's father, former AFL player Ben Hollands, posts on social media pledging to help lift his son up and urging others to check on their friends if they are struggling.

item.title: Wikipedia co-founder Jimmy Wales on AI and trust
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Warwick Thornton and Deborah Mailman on their new film Wolfram
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: ACCC says Woolworths 'contravened' own guardrails for 'Prices Dropped' promotion, court hears
item.description: The consumer watchdog has taken Woolworths to court, claiming shoppers weren't receiving genuine discounts on hundreds of items.

item.title: The Drop music festival still owes ticketholders refunds, six years after cancelled shows
item.description: Ticketholders are still waiting for refunds and answers from The Drop music festival.

item.title: Man sentenced for pretending to shoot people while on Bondi Beach terror attack bridge
item.description: A man who yelled antisemitic profanities while pretending to shoot at people from a pedestrian bridge used by the Bondi Beach terror attack gunmen has been sentenced.

item.title: Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer leaves Trump's cabinet after abuse of power allegations
item.description: The Labor secretary's departure was announced by a White House aide on Monday, local time.

item.title: ACT Greens leader Shane Rattenbury denies quitting over Liberals coalition talks, but they can't have helped
item.description: In announcing his retirement from ACT politics, Greens leader Shane Rattenbury says he is looking forward to finding out what his next career move could be.

item.title: Rebel Wilson requested website describing producer as an 'Indian Ghislaine Maxwell', court hears
item.description: Australian actor Rebel Wilson asked her crisis communications firm to publish a website that accused a producer on her own film of being an "Indian Ghislaine Maxwell", a Sydney court hears.

item.title: More uncounted votes discovered in SA a month after state election
item.description: More uncounted votes have been discovered in two electorates a month on from the South Australian state election, and while the state's Electoral Commission says the ballots will not harm the "integrity" of the count, they have not said how many have been found.

item.title: China opens the gate to more Aussie beef with new export licences for several facilities
item.description: China has granted beef export approval to several new Australian facilities, just months after introducing a range of trade measures to protect its domestic cattle industry.

item.title: Government invests in counter-drone technology citing lessons of Iran war
item.description: The Australian government signs two contracts to develop counter-drone technology, including a laser that can track an object the size of a 10-cent coin travelling at more than 100 km/h.

item.title: El Salvador holds mass trial of nearly 500 alleged MS-13 gang members
item.description: The attorney-general says 486 suspected MS-13 members have faced trial for 47,000 crimes committed between 2012 and 2022.

item.title: Charges for CSL Australia over environmental impacts from Goliath crash with tugboats
item.description: The owner of a bulk cement carrier has been charged with environmental offences following a 2022 crash that sank two tugboats in north-west Tasmania.

item.title: States and territories left in the dark on NDIS savings
item.description: State treasurers say a meeting on NDIS reforms to be announced tomorrow was light on details, leaving states and territories waiting with everyone else to find out how a new round of savings measures will affect the disability scheme — and their budgets.

item.title: Coroner hands down findings into Queensland woman's fatal arson attack on ex-partner
item.description: A coroner has ruled it would have been "extremely difficult" for anyone to predict a mentally unwell woman would murder her ex-partner in an arson attack in south-east Queensland, which also claimed her life.

item.title: Wollongong researchers working to create shark-deterrent surfboard fins
item.description: A multi-million-dollar project run out of the University of Wollongong is working on fitting shark-deterrent technology into surfboard fins following recent shark attacks.

item.title: Tom Boyd says other AFL players are struggling 'under the surface' after Elijah Hollands episode
item.description: Tom Boyd, former AFL footballer turned mental health advocate, says the high-pressure environment of professional football means many players are struggling with mental health issues "under the surface".

item.title: Vanuatu denies it is about to sign a security deal with China as Australia works to ink its own pact
item.description: As Australia presses on to finally sign a key agreement with Vanuatu, reports of a security agreement with China have drawn a fierce response from Port Vila.

item.title: Two teenagers sentenced for roles in Ellenbrook school stabbing, planned as social media 'game'
item.description: Two boys are sentenced for their roles in the stabbing of a 15-year-old boy at a Perth high school, offending the prosecutor said was part of a "game" formulated online.

item.title: Expectant parents and ED patients diverted as two regional hospitals struggle with workforce shortages
item.description: Two regional hospitals, 30 minutes' drive apart, struggle to consistently provide routine medical services, forcing repeated patient diversions.

item.title: CFMEU used former boxers to intimidate contractors at former Toombul shopping site, inquiry hears
item.description: An inquiry into the CFMEU in Queensland has heard the union pressured Toombul shopping centre demolition contractors to hire former kickboxers.

item.title: Former civil servant says there was political pressure to approve Peter Mandelson as UK ambassador to US
item.description: The former head of the Foreign Office said there was "a very, very strong expectation" that Peter Mandelson "needed to be in post and in America as quickly as possible".

item.title: Queensland Tourism Industry Council calls on government for support as tourists cancel bookings
item.description: A Far North Queensland caravan park manager says more people are ringing up to cancel than to book a spot with concerns over fuel prices and availability.

item.title: Premier Coal set to reduce production, cut jobs from Collie mine
item.description: Premier Coal confirms job losses at its Collie operation, with workers set to be informed of the details.

item.title: Captain stranded in Strait of Hormuz tells of being trapped amid Iran war
item.description: For months, seafarers have been stranded in the Strait of Hormuz. Tanker captain Raman Kapoor is speaking out about the fears and uncertainty of his crew.

item.title: Tuesday finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Narco Islands: A special investigation into the Pacific's drug superhighway
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Should wild deer be protected or declared a pest?
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Government backs down on charging older Australians $50/hour for showers
item.description: The decision to scrap the plan to charge older Australians $50/hour for showers was made after months of sustained criticism, including warnings that people were forgoing other care to afford the service.

item.title: Japan overhauls defence export rule to sell arms to 17 countries, including Australia
item.description: Japan has overhauled its decades-long defence export rule, allowing the country to sell lethal weapons internationally amid rising global tensions.

item.title: Live: Iran says Trump's ceasefire extension a 'ploy to buy time' as Vance cancels Pakistan trip
item.description: US President Donald Trump says he is extending the ceasefire with Iran. Follow live.

item.title: Whether it's grief or liberation, family estrangement affects many of us
item.description: Our recent stories about the grief of family estrangement resonated strongly. Here's how some Australians have been affected by it.

item.title: Adelaide Biennial of Australian Art sees how 24 artists working across the country Yield Strength
item.description: The legacy of nuclear testing, environmental degradation and more among the challenges facing Australia, as explored by artists in the Adelaide Biennial of Australian art.

item.title: Travel quiz, April 22: We're off for a trip around the world
item.description: Which city is hosting the first game of the World Cup? What's the name of Egypt's currency?

item.title: Butler urged to end 'limbo' for 500,000 Australians with psychosocial disability in NDIS reforms
item.description: NDIS Minister Mark Butler must end the uncertainty half a million people have been living in when he announces changes to the nation's disability scheme, mental health advocates say.

item.title: Support services nationwide 'buckling under demand' during Sexual Assault Awareness Month
item.description: As Australia marks Sexual Assault Awareness Month, experts and victim-survivors say sexual violence remains pervasive and under-reported.

item.title: Victorians transitioning from gas exacerbates growing problem of undervoltage
item.description: A power company warns that growing electricity demand is creating an undervoltage issue as Victoria's infrastructure struggles to keep up with the transition from gas.

item.title: Closure of AEIOU centres for children with autism after NDIS cuts leaves lives upended
item.description: The nationwide closure of specialist centres has put a spotlight on the adequacy of National Disability Insurance Scheme funding for children with "severe, profound, lifelong" autism.

item.title: Carpooling helps to slash fuel prices, so why aren't more people doing it?
item.description: Despite fuel prices soaring in March, the number of vehicles travelling along a key carpooling lane in Sydney increased by just 1 per cent. A transport expert says more needs to be done to encourage people to share their commutes.

item.title: Junior clubs, former player and neuroscientists welcome new NRL guidelines to limit head knocks but some say it's not enough
item.description: Junior rugby league clubs and neuroscientists have welcomed changes to address growing concerns around injuries but say more should be done to protect children.

item.title: FOI documents reveal Adelaide's COP31 considerations included gift bags and 'flotels'
item.description: A heavily redacted feasibility study into Adelaide's capacity to host the COP31 UN climate summit provides a fleeting glimpse into what was under consideration — including politically fraught floating hotels.

item.title: A rare glimpse inside the services helping NSW women leaving custody get clean
item.description: Guthrie House is one of the few residential rehabilitation services in NSW designed to help women with current involvement with the criminal justice system get back on track.

item.title: Donald Trump extends Iran ceasefire but continues Strait of Hormuz blockade
item.description: US President Donald Trump says he will hold off attacking Iran until Tehran's negotiating teams come up with a unified proposal for a peace deal.

item.title: What 100 years of Australian soil samples in our national archive can tell us about our history and future
item.description: Far from brown dirt, the Australian National Soil Archive houses 100,000 soil samples — some a century old — that represent a slice through time of the ground beneath our feet.

item.title: Australian-first lawsuit over 'extreme heat' risks in remote NT public housing
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Papunya residents living in extreme heat sue NT government over unsafe housing
item.description: Residents of the Central Australian community of Papunya say the scorching hot outback summers are killing their people in unsafe housing. Now they’re suing the Northern Territory government to try to force change.

item.title: Newcastle homebuyers push for reform after off-the-plan development leaves lives in 'limbo'
item.description: Five years after signing contracts and paying their deposits, families say their lives have been put on hold as they wait to move into their "forever homes" in Newcastle.

item.title: Mark Butler announces major NDIS overhaul — as it happened
item.description: NDIS Minister Mark Butler says the scheme was designed to support 410,000 people with a disability, but currently supports 760,000.

item.title: Kyle Flanagan benched as Dean Young names first Dragons NRL team, fires assistant coach Michael Ennis
item.description: Dean Young names the first team of his tenure as Dragons coach, dropping Kyle Flanagan to the bench and sacking assistant and attack coach Michael Ennis.

item.title: Chocolate-box violin inspires teen to retrace his Anzac relative's World War I footsteps
item.description: Ernest Sidney Pilcher died of his wounds in France on June 25, 1918. This Anzac Day, inspired by a remarkable family relic, his great-great-great-nephew will deliver a eulogy at his gravesite.

item.title: Live: ASX tumbles as Cochlear shares crash 40pc to 10-year low
item.description: One of Australia's biggest companies, Cochlear, has rattled investors by slashing its profit outlook, while the broader market drops. Follow live.

item.title: Queensland Maroons name Tarryn Aiken, Ali Brigginshaw replacements for Women's State of Origin
item.description: With stalwart halves Ali Brigginshaw and Tarryn Aiken unavailable for selection, the Maroons name a new-look Queensland side for the first Women's State of Origin game in Newcastle.

item.title: Fire conditions on a 'knife edge' in Queensland's south as burn window tightens
item.description: A Queensland Rural Fire Service superintendent says fire conditions in parts of the state are on the edge after receiving very little rain this wet season.

item.title: Southern NSW family's push for affordable eczema medication so kids can have a normal childhood
item.description: The cost of relief for kids under 12 with severe eczema is $1,600 a month, but a push for PBS funding is looking to change that.

item.title: US authorities probe possible ChatGPT involvement in university shooting
item.description: US authorities are investigating if there was any criminal behaviour involving interactions between Open AI's ChatGPT app and a man accused of killing two people and injuring six others in a shooting at Florida State University.

item.title: Leicester City relegated to League One 10 years after Premier League title
item.description: Ten years after a fairytale Premier League title and five years since winning the FA Cup, Leicester City is destined for the third tier of English football.

item.title: Remote NT residents evacuated to Batchelor say security 'treating us like prisoners'
item.description: Flood evacuees from remote Northern Territory communities say they are being "treated like prisoners" by security guards working at their emergency accommodation centre.

item.title: Iranian propaganda accounts have been generating AI Lego-style videos, such as this one.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Meme warfare and 'slopaganda' are defining the AI-era Iran war
item.description: AI-generated Lego memes criticising the conflict in the Middle East and mocking Donald Trump are attracting a large audience online as a new front opens in the Iran war.

item.title: 'Terrifying' experience for Bunbury family as wild storm rips roof off their home
item.description: A woman describes her "terrifying" ordeal after a powerful cold front which sounded like a "jet plane" tore the roof off her family home while she was inside it on Monday night.

item.title: Boy, 15, among five people charged over alleged kidnapping of rapper's bodyguard in Sydney's west
item.description: Four men and a teenage boy are set to front court today over the alleged assault and kidnapping of a bodyguard for rapper and alleged Alameddine associate Ali Younes.

item.title: Iran daily briefing: Fragile ceasefire extended, along with anxiety and confusion
item.description: The Iran war ceasefire has been extended but no-one is quite sure on the new deadline, Middle East correspondent Matthew Doran writes.

item.title: Newly revealed uncounted votes from SA election number 'in the order of dozens'
item.description: The number of newly revealed uncounted ballot papers from the March state election is believed to be in the "order of dozens", South Australia's Attorney-General says.

item.title: Three weeks of wages guaranteed for Liberty Bell Bay smelter workers in state, federal deal
item.description: A short-term rescue package announced by the Tasmanian and federal governments will guarantee the wages of workers at the troubled Liberty Bell Bay smelter.

item.title: About 100 GPs now qualified to diagnose ADHD in SA with calls to expand to regions
item.description: About 100 South Australian GPs are now qualified to diagnose ADHD and provide treatment, with the hope of that number doubling by the end of the year, with one patient describing her diagnosis through her GP as "life-changing".

item.title: Department staff to face misconduct proceedings over triple killer living with foster kids
item.description: Some Department of Communities and Justice staff members will face misconduct proceedings after a bungle that saw two foster children living with triple killer Regina Arthurell earlier this year.

item.title: Meet the eco-venture turning European carp into popular dog treats
item.description: Introducing the European carp to Australian waterways is an ecological disaster that continues to worsen, but an Adelaide couple have found a novel use for the pest.

item.title: Australian golf junior hoping to test his skills at Canadian invitational
item.description: Alby Weyden was two years old when he became obsessed with golf. Four years on, his family hopes to raise enough money for him to compete in the Canadian Invitational.

item.title: The sound of a night parrot recorded at a new location in southwest Queensland
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Critically endangered night parrots detected in south-west Queensland
item.description: Ecologists say thousands of hours of audio recordings have detected night parrot calls in south-west Queensland.

item.title: Michael Jackson gets the formulaic musical biopic he doesn't deserve
item.description: Spanning from the Jackson 5's 1960s discovery through to the height of his fame in the late 80s, Michael skates over the more challenging aspects of the King of Pop.

item.title: Gaza needs $US71 billion in next decade for recovery, UN and EU report finds
item.description: More than $US71 billion will be needed over the next 10 years for recovery and reconstruction in Gaza, according to a new report by the European Union and the United Nations.

item.title: Charities struggle to stay open as food insecurity rises
item.description: Charities that provide food say they are running at full capacity, forcing some to close and others to rethink how they deliver meals.

item.title: Canberra police hunting for man who allegedly assaulted jogger and approached child in separate incidents
item.description: A statement from ACT Policing said they had linked the same individual through DNA analysis to the assault of a woman last month and to an incident involving a child earlier in the year.

item.title: Timorese remember the child heroes who helped Anzacs in World War II
item.description: Incredible bonds were formed between Australian commandos and Timorese boys during World War II. The White Australia policy meant they had to say goodbye when the Anzacs departed.

item.title: Box jellyfish sighting in Dampier
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Deadly box jellyfish swarm recorded off Pilbara coast
item.description: Almost 100 deadly box jellyfish have gathered off a port in WA's north. Scientists say they likely belong to an unnamed species.

item.title: Mice taking over paddocks
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Farmers fear potential mouse plague as CSIRO warns of high numbers for SA
item.description: As if the cost of diesel and the lack of fertiliser were not enough, South Australia's farmers are also facing the prospect of a mouse plague.

item.title: Jason Moloney eyeing top-level return, Conor Wallace still waiting for chance
item.description: Jason Moloney knows the path back to the summit of world boxing is paved with pain, but he will take another step back in Brisbane on Thursday night.

item.title: Millie Elliott returns, Teagan Berry to debut as NSW Sky Blues name Women's State of Origin I team
item.description: The Sky Blues welcome back Millie Elliott for the 2025 Women's State of Origin series opener, while Emma Verran's pregnancy opens up a spot for Teagan Berry to make her debut.

item.title: Admiral Samuel Paparo calls for more US vessels in Indo-Pacific
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: US commander says Australia is ready for AUKUS subs, warns of 'increasingly aggressive' China
item.description: The US's Indo-Pacific commander praises work to upgrade Australia's AUKUS base, but says he needs more naval firepower in the region as China rapidly expands its military.

item.title: Former Queensland industrial relations minister Grace Grace threatened developers, CFMEU inquiry hears
item.description: The Commission of Inquiry into the CFMEU is told Grace Grace "made it clear" she expected the developers to cooperate with the union, or she would consider terminating their contract.

item.title: NSW planning panel approves new Orange greyhound track
item.description: The council and a community group fought against it, but Orange is set to get a new greyhound racing track after the project was approved by the Western Regional Planning Panel.

item.title: After 27 years and 20 losses, can South Sydney finally find their first win in Melbourne?
item.description: The Rabbitohs have never won a game in Melbourne in almost three decades of trying but they might never get a better chance than this weekend.

item.title: Man accused of killing Labor identity Tim Picton pleads guilty over Kalamunda assault
item.description: A man who allegedly killed Labor identity Tim Picton in Perth admits assaulting another man in Kalamunda just days earlier.

item.title: Edge Early Learning CEO denies fast growth to blame for SA childcare centre issues
item.description: The head of Edge Early Learning says he is conducting a "whole of business transformation project" after being the subject of compliance action over inadequate supervision and other issues at its childcare centres in Adelaide.

item.title: 160,000 people to be kicked off NDIS as government overhauls eligibility test
item.description: Health and NDIS Minister Mark Butler unveiled a list of cost-saving measures designed to return the scheme to sustainable levels in a major speech to the National Press Club.

item.title: Fake document claims emerge in Corporate Travel's $240 million UK overcharging scandal
item.description: Brisbane-based Corporate Travel Management has detailed a laundry list of concerns about a ballooning scandal, including reliance on potentially fake documents.

item.title: Body found in New Zealand after deluge causes major flooding in Wellington
item.description: Police have found the body of a man as floodwaters recede in New Zealand's capital and the clean-up begins.

item.title: Ballarat man bailed for dozens of offences including culpable driving arrested at the wheel in Canadian
item.description: A man released on bail for about 60 charges, including two counts of culpable driving causing death, has been arrested at the wheel near Ballarat.

item.title: How these households divide and conquer mealtimes
item.description: From share houses to solo living, this is how three households manage the cooking differently.

item.title: Netball Australia CEO Stacey West steps down as sport prepares for new era
item.description: Stacey West will step down as CEO of Netball Australia at the end of this week, ending a two-and-a-half-year tenure that has reshaped the sport in this country.

item.title: Dorrigo Anzac war memorial vandalised in drunken incident repaired
item.description: A Laurieton stonemason rushed to the task of repairing a vandalised marble rifle on a NSW Mid North Coast war memorial.

item.title: Assault charges against Offspring actor Alicia Gardiner dropped by Melbourne prosecutors
item.description: The actor was accused of "breaking the girl code" by grabbing the breast of a staffer at Parliament House in Melbourne during a pro-Palestine demonstration two years ago.

item.title: Ampol has received $25 million in funding to produce renewable diesel. How can it be used, and will it boost supply?
item.description: The Queensland government has given $25 million to Ampol so it can produce renewable diesel in a bid to boost domestic fuel supplies.
